mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.2% as of Nov 2018 reflects recent rate cuts
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points within 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of Nov 2018, near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening reversal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as sentiment shifts.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513531 million as of Nov 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, indicating stagflationary pressure that compresses real corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.05% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.39 and CPIAUCSL at 252.88 as of Nov 2018
failure_condition: Mean 20-day returns during stagflationary episodes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL) has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply, signaling reduced construction activity and future residential investment, which dampens aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 8% month-over-month or 3-month moving average declines > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1228 thousand units as of Oct 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances despite housing decline signal, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and 60-day forward returns is < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200k monthly additions while unemployment remains below 4%, signaling tight labor market that increases wage pressures and forces central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as rate hike expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149893 thousand and UNRATE at 3.7% as of Nov 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightness with policy tightening risk
failure_condition: Mean 20-day returns during tight labor market episodes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal (PAYEMS growth AND low UNRATE) has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic expansion and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18671.65 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q3 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of quarters with sub-2% GDP growth, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, signaling real rates turning negative and forcing future monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 252.88 and FEDFUNDS at 2.2% as of Nov 2018
regime_hint: negative real rates with tightening cycle ahead
failure_condition: Mean 20-day returns during negative real rate episodes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS spread) has Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
